page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier memory performance, with uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 479.59 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 235.55 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a top-tier bzip3 decompression rate of 673.46 GB/sec and strong multi-core Geekbench and stress-ng performance. Database and web serving capabilities are also notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 133.96 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 118.02 GB/sec.

While the instance delivers strong multi-core and memory-bound performance, single-core CPU performance falls into the average tier. Additionally, LLM text generation for smaller models is relatively poor, though medium and large model text generation speeds remain strong. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides high resource density and memory bandwidth per unit of compute, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances for workloads that do not require local NVMe storage.

Given its high memory capacity, 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and strong database benchmark results, the r8gn.16xlarge is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the instance relies on network-attached storage and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached reads at 479.59 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-threaded performance, including 133.96 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core performance and small-model LLM text generation are average to poor, its high memory density and memory bandwidth make it highly efficient for memory-bound workloads. The r8gn.16xlarge is suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, built for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring high-bandwidth memory and dedicated physical cores for demanding database and data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz on AWS Graviton ARMv9 architecture
512.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier uncached read and write bandwidth
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for network and EBS connectivity
Top-tier database performance with 133.96 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Suited for large-scale databases, multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving
